text: ↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇱🇮🇷🇺🇸🇱🇧 Israeli Army Radio: Behind the scenes, Iran and the U.S. continue negotiations for a deal to end the war, with the U.S. imposing a naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz to pressure Tehran. However, Iran has publicly committed to not accepting…" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇺🇸 Israeli Channel 12: The United States is pressing Israel to fully cease fire in Lebanon, starting in the coming days. Washington proposes a temporary ceasefire with an ultimatum to renew fighting within a week – similar to the Iran deal. Israel has not yet decided. The IDF is preparing for a potential stop order from the political echelon and is accelerating plans that may be shelved in southern Lebanon. The security cabinet will meet this evening, with some ministers pushing to resume strikes in Beirut and north of the Litani River. @wfwitness

🇮🇱 ⚡️ 🇱🇧 Conflicting Reports on Potential Lebanon Ceasefire "No decision has yet been made on a ceasefire in Lebanon." In a follow-up report from KAN News, Zionist source acknowledged ongoing contacts but emphasized that fighting would continue as long as Hezbollah keeps firing: "There are contacts – the fire will not stop as long as Hezbollah is firing."

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇱🇺🇸🇱🇧🇱🇧 Contrary to popular reports, a senior Israeli official told Kan that no decision had been made on a ceasefire with Lebanon. The IDF said the decision rests with the political echelon. Another Israeli source said contacts for a ceasefire were underway…" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇱🇧 Israeli Army Radio: Behind the scenes, Iran and the U.S. continue negotiations for a deal to end the war, with the U.S. imposing a naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz to pressure Tehran. However, Iran has publicly committed to not accepting any agreement that does not include a ceasefire in Lebanon—a demand reiterated by a senior Iranian official today to Al-Maydeen. The U.S. has already pressured Israel to halt strikes in deep Lebanon (Beirut and the Bekaa) for the past week, but Iran is now demanding a full ceasefire across all of Lebanon. The key question remains unanswered: Will Iran succeed in forcing the U.S. to impose a ceasefire in Lebanon on Israel, thereby tying the end of the war in Iran to the end of the war in Lebanon? @wfwitness
⚡ 🇮🇱 Drone alert sirens are active in the Confrontation Line region, Northern Israel. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇮🇷🇱🇧🇮🇱 Al Mayadeen: A senior Iranian political‑security source told the Lebanese network that a ceasefire in Lebanon will take effect from tonight. The truce will last one week, extending until the end of the ceasefire between Iran and the United States.…" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 Contrary to popular reports, a senior Israeli official told Kan that no decision had been made on a ceasefire with Lebanon. The IDF said the decision rests with the political echelon. Another Israeli source said contacts for a ceasefire were underway and a proposal was conveyed at the U.S. request, but the fire would not stop as long as Hezbollah continued to launch attacks. The Israeli security cabinet is set to meet this evening to discuss the proposal. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇸🇾 🇸🇾 🌟 Drug filled stashes launched from Syrian Government Forces towards Jordan. Statement issued by Swaida's National Guard Command: At 10:00 PM last night, National Guard units detected the launch of balloons carrying a pre-prepared cargo from areas in the eastern Daraa countryside, controlled by the terrorist Damascus regime. The balloons were headed towards the northeastern countryside of the Jabal al-Druze region, reaching the eastern desert. These areas are known for drug smuggling gangs operating under the supervision of the terrorist regime forces. During field surveillance, it was determined that the balloons were filled with helium gas and used to transport a shipment of narcotics. It was also discovered that they were booby-trapped and designed to detonate if they deviated from their designated course. Accordingly, National Guard units targeted and downed them before they reached their destination. The balloons landed in agricultural land between the villages of Amra and Al-Hayat in the northern countryside of the mountain. The sector command was immediately notified, and engineering personnel went to the site, where they successfully dismantled the mines attached to the shipment and seized a bag containing fifteen kilograms of Captagon pills. The seized materials will be handed over to the relevant authorities for destruction according to established procedures. The National Guard Command confirms that this operation proves the involvement of the terrorist regime in Damascus in the trade and smuggling of narcotics and the development of new methods to smuggle them across the borders. @wfwitness

🇱🇧 🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 A high-level Iranian political-security source to Al Mayadeen: – Following monitoring and pressure from Iran, a ceasefire in Lebanon will be approved starting tonight. – The ceasefire will last for one week and will extend until the end of the ceasefire period between Iran and the United States. – It is possible that Netanyahu, acting as a disruptive actor, may move again to undermine this agreement. Disclaimer: This report comes exclusively from Al Mayadeen’s Iranian source. Zionist regime haven't yet confirmed the ceasefire, and the situation on the ground remains fluid.

Disclose.tv JUST IN - An Iranian official tells Al-Mayadeen news that a ceasefire will take effect in Lebanon starting tonight for 1 week, until the end of the 2 week ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran — Times of Israel tweet

WarTranslated Péter Magyar posted a video poking fun at Orbán. The President of Hungary was showing Magyar his office in the Sándor Palace. At that moment, Orbán was spotted on the balcony of the Prime Minister’s Office next door, reading something with great concentration. Magyar jokingly suggested that it was either his farewell speech or a fresh statement from Donald Trump. tweet

This would only strengthen that influence. HEZBOLLAH STARTED THIS, ISRAEL SHOULD FINISH THIS. Open Source Intel Very significant. A senior U.S. delegation visited Somaliland yesterday. The commander of U.S. Africa Command met with Somaliland's chief of staff. Somaliland officials said after the meeting that "control near the Bab el-Mandeb Strait and the Red Sea would significantly change the U.S. approach to dealing with the Houthis and Iran." Somaliland expressed support for the U.S. and Israel using its territory against the Houthis. This comes a day after Israeli reports of concern that the Houthis may attempt to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait. N12. - Open Source Intel tweet

He stated that Israel is only seeking an image and remains unable to plant its feet in front-line villages despite alleged "massacres against civilians." He criticised the Lebanese government for making concessions to the "enemy" and failing to appreciate the resistance, calling for a comprehensive ceasefire and full implementation of the previous agreement. @wfwitness

When the session convened, all members voted with clarity and insight, and Khamenei’s vote was the highest by a wide margin. @wfwitness

Somaliland officials said after the meeting that "control near the Bab el-Mandeb Strait and the Red Sea would significantly change the U.S. approach to dealing with the Houthis and Iran." Somaliland expressed support for the U.S. and Israel using its territory against the Houthis. This comes a day after Israeli reports of concern that the Houthis may attempt to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait. N12. tweet

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡🇺🇸🇪🇺🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump dismissed a European plan to open the Strait of Hormuz without U.S. involvement, saying: "We're doing it now — the ships are coming back and forth," and questioning how Europe could take such a position after relying on…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 U.S. President Donald Trump said he will fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell if he does not leave "on time," stating: "I've held back firing him. I've wanted to fire him." Trump linked the threat to an ongoing probe into the cost of a Federal Reserve building renovation, which he said was projected at $25 million but may reach $4 billion, saying he would not drop the investigation. "Whether it's incompetence, corruption or both, I think you have to find out." Trump also said he believes his preferred Fed Chair nominee Kevin Walsh would be "great," and suggested that outgoing Senator Tom Tillis — who he said "quit" — would not want the legacy of blocking the nomination. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡🇺🇸🇮🇹🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump said the U.S. no longer has the same relationship with Italy, implying that countries which declined to support U.S. operations against Iran have faced a diplomatic consequence. "Negative — anybody that turned us down…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 🇪🇺 🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump dismissed a European plan to open the Strait of Hormuz without U.S. involvement, saying: "We're doing it now — the ships are coming back and forth," and questioning how Europe could take such a position after relying on American support. Trump also reiterated his view that the U.S. should control Greenland to counter Russia and China, and said the country should not be "paying trillions of dollars to NATO." @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡🇺🇸🇬🇧 U.S. President Donald Trump attributed the UK's growth forecast downgrade to its energy and immigration policies, saying Britain needs to "stop with the windmills" and reopen North Sea oil fields. "They have the North Sea in Aberdeen, Scotland — one…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 🇮🇹 🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump said the U.S. no longer has the same relationship with Italy, implying that countries which declined to support U.S. operations against Iran have faced a diplomatic consequence. "Negative — anybody that turned us down for helping with this Iran situation, we do not have the same relationship," he said. Trump noted that Italy is heavily dependent on oil passing through the Strait of Hormuz, appearing to underscore the stakes of its position. @wfwitness
🇺🇸 🏁 Why are we spending hundreds of billions of dollars a year on NATO if they’re not going to be with us? - Trump 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Trump said Iran wants to reach a deal "very badly," expressing…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 🇬🇧 U.S. President Donald Trump attributed the UK's growth forecast downgrade to its energy and immigration policies, saying Britain needs to "stop with the windmills" and reopen North Sea oil fields. "They have the North Sea in Aberdeen, Scotland — one of the most incredible oil fields anywhere in the world — and they closed it," Trump said, blaming environmentalists for making extraction "impossible" and claiming oil companies had asked him to intervene. Trump also cited immigration as a compounding problem, saying what Europe has done on immigration "is horrible." @wfwitness
🇺🇸 🇮🇹 Maria Bartiromo: Do we still have the same relationship right now with Italy and Meloni? Trump: No, she has been negative. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

"I had to go to a war. I had to go and fight — some people would call it a…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump said he views the conflict with Iran as "very close to over," adding that if U.S. forces withdrew now, "it would take them 20 years to rebuild that country." Trump said Iran wants to reach a deal "very badly," expressing confidence that direct talks would yield an agreement. He also noted that the U.S. has received "zero" support from NATO throughout the operation. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡🇺🇸🇨🇳 U.S. President Donald Trump declined to outline a specific response to a recent FBI-attributed Chinese cyber attack, saying "it is what it is" and acknowledging that both countries conduct cyber operations against each other. They're…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump said he expects the U.S. economy to grow this year despite what he described as a temporary diversion caused by military operations against Iran. "I had to go to a war. I had to go and fight — some people would call it a war, some people call it a military operation. It was easy, because we have the greatest military in the world," Trump said, adding that he acted to prevent Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon. "If I didn't do that, right now you would have Iran with a nuclear weapon," he said. @wfwitness

"Amazing 2 Days" Trump Teases Deal as Iran Threatenes Gulf U.S. President Donald Trump said Iran talks could resume, telling the world to watch out for "amazing 2 days" as US forces claim enforcement of a naval blockade on Iranian ports. Iran has also threatened total blockade of Gulf port activity if the U.S. insists on naval blockade. READ MORE

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡🇺🇸🇨🇳🇮🇷 U.S. President Donald Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping wrote him a letter asserting that China is not supplying weapons to Iran, in response to a letter Trump said he sent after receiving reports of such transfers. "I wrote him a letter asking…" ⚡ 🇺🇸 🇨🇳 U.S. President Donald Trump declined to outline a specific response to a recent FBI-attributed Chinese cyber attack, saying "it is what it is" and acknowledging that both countries conduct cyber operations against each other. "China's China. They're never easy, but we're doing great with China," he said. Trump pointed to his 100% tariff on Chinese cars as evidence of his hardline stance, contrasting the U.S. approach with Europe, which he said is "being inundated with Chinese cars." @wfwitness
